title: Labor Secures Second Term In SA's 2026 State Election Race | 10 News
description: Labor once again retains power in South Australia, as Peter Malinauskas secures a second term in a landslide win for his party.  They went into the election with 29 seats, and they have won at least 30 – which will be a record for a Labor government in South Australian parliament.   Hear what Premier-elect Peter Malinauskas and Liberal Leader Ashton Hurn had to say. description: It turns out one of the world's most powerful warships' biggest weaknesses could be a fitness app.  A young French naval officer has accidentally revealed the location of his country's largest aircraft carrier after logging his workout on the Strava app.  10’s Late News explains. title: Two Skiers Killed, 25 Trapped After Avalanche In Italian Alps | 10 News
description: Two skiers have died and five others were injured after an avalanche in the Italian Alps on Saturday.  A total of 25 skiers were caught at an altitude of about 2,400 metres when it struck around 11:40 am (local time) on the slopes of Hohe Ferse, close to the border with Austria.  A major rescue operation was launched, deploying six helicopters and around 80 rescuers.  This season has seen an unusually high number of deaths from avalanches with 127 killed so far. title: Sky High Fuel Prices Could Stay For Years As Iran War Shifts Targets To Nuclear Sites | 10 News+
description: Experts have issued a new fuel warning that the hefty prices across Australia could stay for years despite Donald Trump claiming the war with Iran is nearly over.  It comes as nuclear sites on both sides came under attack as the US President set the clock on a deadline to re-open the key oil shipping route.  The grim outlook on the future of petrol prices has some convinced that cheaper public transport could be the solution.
